commit.message:
fix(ngrx): replace Promise chains with Observable pattern in effects, fix switchMap on mutations, add toast error notifications (#117)

* fix(effects): replace Promise chains with from()+catchError(), fix switchMap on mutations, add toast notifications

- Convert all Promise .then().catch() chains in board, board-list, board-template,
  activity, and user-profile effects to the safe from()+pipe(map(),catchError())
  Observable pattern. This prevents edge-case errors from escaping the inner
  observable and killing the actions$ stream (which silently dropped the current
  action on NgRx re-subscription).
- Fix task.effects.ts: change switchMap -> mergeMap for addTask$, updateTask$,
  deleteTask$ so rapid/programmatic double-dispatches no longer cancel in-flight
  form saves.
- Inject ToastrService into all effects files and show error toasts in every
  failure path (API exceptions via catchError and logical failures via map when
  the API returns null/false).

IDs directly from build timeline 'Send to Helix' task records.

Add write operations gated by ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE (#12)

* Add write operations gated by ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE

Implement the full deposit write workflow, all behind an explicit opt-in:

  ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE=true   (or '1') enables write tools
  ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE=false  (default) — write tools not registered

## New tools (only registered when write is enabled)

Deposition CRUD:
  create_deposition   POST /api/deposit/depositions
  get_deposition      GET  /api/deposit/depositions/:id
  update_deposition   PUT  /api/deposit/depositions/:id  (metadata JSON)
  delete_deposition   DELETE /api/deposit/depositions/:id (drafts only)

File management:
  upload_file             PUT to bucket URL; accepts utf-8 or base64 content
  delete_deposition_file  DELETE /api/deposit/depositions/:id/files/:file_id

Deposition actions:
  publish_deposition   .../actions/publish
  edit_deposition      .../actions/edit  (unlock published record)
  discard_deposition   .../actions/discard
  new_version          .../actions/newversion

## Safety design

- Write tools are not registered at all when ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE is false,
  so AI agents never see them unless the operator has opted in.
- Each handler also checks the flag independently (defence-in-depth).
- All write methods call requireAuth() — clear error if key is missing.
- docker-compose.yml exposes ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE (default: false).

## Tests (test/zenodo-write.test.ts, 19 tests)

- Gate: write tools absent in tool list when disabled
- Gate: write tools present when ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE=true
- Read-only tools still present when write is enabled
- 10 defence-in-depth handler tests (each write tool returns isError when flag off)
- 2 not-authenticated tests (write enabled, no API key)
- 4 live sandbox round-trip tests (create → upload → update → delete),
  activated only when ZENODO_SANDBOX_API_KEY is set

CI: passes ZENODO_SANDBOX_API_KEY secret and sets ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE
accordingly.

38/38 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: enforce boundaries in all no-profile fallback paths

Sweep 2 — four boundary gaps closed:

1. PolishEngine.shouldShowPrompt: maxHeatOverride used a falsy check
   (data.maxHeatOverride && ...) so a value of 0 was silently ignored.
   Changed to (data.maxHeatOverride != null && ...) for strict semantics.

2. PromptsScreen: when getContentProfile() threw, deckPrompts fell back
   to raw ALL_BUNDLED with no boundary filtering. Now loads rawBoundaries
   in parallel and applies a sync filter (applyRawBoundaryFilter) so
   hiddenCategories, pausedEntries, hideSpicy, and maxHeatOverride are
   always respected, even if the full profile is unavailable.

3. PromptLibraryScreen: same pattern — filteredPrompts fell back to
   the raw prompts state. Same fix applied via applyRawBoundaryFilter.

4. DateNightScreen: when getContentProfile() failed, both strictProfileBase
   and fallbackProfileBase fell through to unfiltered allDates, allowing
   paused dates and heat-capped content to surface. Now loads rawBoundaries
   alongside the profile and filters the no-profile path accordingly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

security: clarify legacy PIN hash migration path in pinHash.js

are 32 hex chars, making practical collisions infeasible for 4-digit
